pattern: Filter a singly linked list in one pass, using a hash set of forbidden values so each membership test is constant time and pointers are relinked around removed nodes.
hints: Put the array values into a set first; you need fast membership tests, not a scan per node.
Walk the list with a previous pointer. When the current node's value is in the set, skip it by pointing the previous node's next at the current node's next.
A dummy head node placed before the real head removes the special case of deleting the original first node; return dummy.next at the end.

edge_inputs.description: Statement example 1: remove several leading values
Statement example 2: repeated removed value scattered through list
Statement example 3: no value matches, list unchanged
Single-node list that survives (nums cannot match the only node per problem guarantee)
Head node must be removed; only the tail survives
Removed values interleaved with survivors, last node removed

stress.description: Random list and nums over a small value domain so many removals happen; one survivor guaranteed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    # guarantee at least one survivor: force a value outside the nums set
    forbidden = sorted(set(rng.randint(1, 40) for _ in range(15)))
    safe = 100000
    vals[rng.randint(0, n - 1)] = safe
    return [forbidden, vals]

stress.description: Large list, sparse removals (nums tiny relative to value range)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    forbidden = sorted(set(rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(5)))
    # ensure a survivor exists
    fset = set(forbidden)
    safe = 1
    while safe in fset:
        safe += 1
    vals[0] = safe
    return [forbidden, vals]
